title: Theory and Techniques of Ballet: Intermediate
description: A continuation of Dance 711.

title: Mentored Dance Practice I
description: An intensive course in physical practice designed to develop understanding of diverse forms of dance practices, allowing students to experience a wide range of contemporary and/or hybrid dance forms currently in studio practice.

title: Mentored Dance Practice II
description: A deepening of skills from DANCE 717. A physical practice designed to develop understanding of diverse forms of dance practices, allowing students to experience a wide range of contemporary and/or hybrid dance forms.

title: Alexander Technique, Developmental Movement and Dance
description: A hands-on exploration of the application of the Alexander Technique to advanced dance training through developmental movement.

title: Alexander Technique Practicum
description: Continuation of Dance 721 focusing on a more in-depth study and application of Alexander Technique for advanced dancers.

title: Pilates Technique and Applications
description: Use of Pilates method to understand and articulate essential concepts and theories of movement, and refine dance technique.

title: Somatic Pedagogy and Practice
description: Application of the pedagogical theories and practical strategies of The Feldenkrais Method® to any movement-focused situation, from dance to music or athletics.

title: Improvisation 1
description: Techniques and structures for improvisation--the development of processes for evolving movement material from an exploration of the dynamics of inter and intra-relationships, and environment.

title: Improvisation 2
description: A continuation of Dance 733.

title: Dynamic Analysis of Movement I
description: Exploration of personal patterning and discovery of movement sources at the body level. Incorporates material from bartenieff fundamentals, feldenkrais, and sweigard.

title: Dynamic Analysis of Movement II
description: A continuation of Dance 743. Advanced body level work, application of bartenieff fundamentals to dance technique.

title: Practices of Movement Analysis
description: Theory and movement practice exploring methods for analyzing movement (cultural, kinetic, dynamic, expressive) for use in performance, teaching, and choreography.

title: Dance Pedagogy of Physical Practice
description: Philosophy and methods of teaching physical practices of dance in higher education, K-12 and community engaged settings. Students research culturally relevant methodologies and content; design and present material to develop pedagogical approaches to physical practice.

title: Survey of Dance Literature and Bibliography
description: Lecture, research, and discussion of the major English language dance reference works, and of dance research publications.

title: Dance in Secondary Education and College
description: Methods and philosophy of teaching dance in the secondary school. Theory and practice in solving problems arising in practical teaching situations. Observation of dance lessons.

title: History of Renaissance and Baroque Dance
description: Lecture, discussion of readings of the periods (in translation). Emphasis also upon performing some of the period dances.

title: American Concert Dance of the 20th and 21st Century
description: Graduate seminar in special topics of American concert dance of the twentieth and twentieth first century.

title: Valuing and Evaluation of Dance
description: Observing and describing dancing and choreography and shaping values.

title: Theories of the Body
description: Introduction to multiple perspectives and theories on the body and embodied performance to examine how human bodies are framed and deployed for cultural and political purposes and how they circulate or are constrained.

title: Repertoire and Ensemble
description: Practicum in group performance in dance department productions, touring groups, and performances related to the dance department. Includes original works from classical and contemporary repertoire.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual faculty and student will define the teaching/learning contract for the semester and agree on the credit.

title: Graduate Choreographic Research Seminar I
description: Development of choreographic practice in seminars exploring theories, methodologies, and embodied scholarship within the dancemaking practice.

title: Graduate Choreographic Research Seminar II
description: Exploring a greater depth and complexity of choreographic works with a focus on the development of choreographic practice in seminars exploring theories, methodologies, and embodied scholarship within the dancemaking practice.

title: Sound Design for Dance
description: This course explores the theoretical, technical, and creative work of sound design as it relates to the contemporary dance artist.

title: Research Methodology for Dance
description: Lecture, formulation of research proposal, research and write-up of a dance research problem.

title: Applied Anatomy for Dance
description: Study of human bony and neuromuscular structures in theory and movement. Lec only for 2 cr; lec & lab for 3 cr.

title: Field History: Contemporary Dance Festivals
description: Research, reading, attendance and documentation on selected festivals/series events. Pre-study of choreography/performance style, background, influences, artistic themes. Group discussions and evaluations.

title: Developing Performance Quality
description: Studies in dynamics of specific choreography.

title: Candidate for Degree
description: Available for graduate students who must meet minimum credit load requirement.

title: Special Workshops:
description: Workshops and graduate seminars presented by guest artists and faculty on specialized topics.

title: Dance Final Thesis/Project
description: Final demonstration of craft and artistry mastered in program, when completion approved by project committee.

title: Introduction to Medical Imaging
description: Introduction to Diagnostic Medical Imaging modalities with a special emphasis on Radiologic Technology and Diagnostic Medical Sonography.

title: Diagnostic Medical Imaging Fundamentals
description: Introduction to physics as it relates to Diagnostic Medical Imaging. Students will explore the fundamental principles of physics and its application to Medical Imaging equipment and instrumentation.

title: Imaging Procedures I
description: Radiographic anatomy and procedures related to upper and lower extremities, chest, abdomen, and barium studies. Pediatric imaging is discussed.

title: Seminar in Radiography I
description: Basic concepts of patient care, including consideration for the cultural, physical and psychological needs of the patient and family.

title: Imaging Procedures II
description: Radiographic anatomy and procedures for the bony thorax, vertebral column, and iodinated studies will be covered. Pediatric imaging for each procedure will be discussed.

title: Imaging Procedures III
description: Radiographic anatomy and procedures for the cranium and facial bones; pediatric imaging for each procedure will be discussed.

title: Introduction to Sonography
description: Introduction to Diagnostic Medical Sonography with an overview of basic terminology, techniques, and equipment.

title: Abdominal Sonography I
description: Anatomy, physiology, pathology, and pathophysiology of the abdominal vessels and organs as they relate to sonography.

title: Abdominal Sonography II
description: Anatomy, physiology, pathology, and pathophysiology of the kidneys and abdominal organs as they relate to sonography.

title: Sonography Clinical Education I
description: Introduction to sonography scanning and development of clinical skills.

title: Abdominal Sonography III
description: Normal gross, cross-sectional, relational and sonographic anatomy of superficial structures including male pelvis, thyroid,  parathyroid glands. Pathologic findings are correlated with causative etiologies utilizing lab values in conjunction with ultrasound protocols.

title: Obstetrics/Gynecology Sonography I
description: Normal gross, relational and sonographic anatomy of the female pelvic region. Physiology and related laboratory and clinical findings for disease processes are discussed.

title: Obstetrics/Gynecology Sonography II
description: Gravid uterus and fetal development. Gestational dating methods, embryology, and maternal and fetal anatomy.

title: Obstetrics/Gynecology Sonography III
description: Fetal anomalies, disease processes, and pathologies. Various fetal and maternal pathologies explained in sonographic terms.

title: Sonographic Principles and Instrumentation
description: Characteristics of sound and mechanism of sonographic image production and display. Potential biological effects. Safety and mathematical principles.

title: Sonography Clinical Education II
description: Clinical practicum rotations including abdominal and OB/GYN imaging, and vascular technology.

title: Specialized Sonography
description: Embryology, structure, physiology, and pathology of the thyroid, breast, and testicle. Normal and abnormal gross, cross-sectional, and relational sonographic anatomy.

title: Sonography Clinical Education III
description: Clinical practicum rotations including abdominal and OB/GYN imaging, and vascular technology.

title: Abdominal Sonography IV
description: Anatomy, physiology, pathology and pathophysiology of the adult prostate, penis, and GI tract and the pediatric abdomen and neck as seen on normal and abnormal sonograms.

title: Introduction to Breast Imaging
description: This course focuses on the normal gross, cross-sectional, relational, and sonographic anatomy of the breast and associated structures.

title: Cardiovascular Principles I
description: Introduction to cardiac physiology, mechanical events, left ventricle function indicators, timing of mitral and aortic flow, auscultation, phonocardiography, hemodynamics and Doppler applications of echocardiography.

title: Clinical Education I - Echocardiography
description: Clinical practicum rotations including adult echocardiography.

title: Cardiovascular Physiology
description: This course will discuss cardiac physiology and electrophysiology, ausculation, mechanical and electrical events of the heart, chronotropic and inotropic forces, and components of the cardiac cycle including IVRT, IVCT, systole, preload, and afterload.

title: Embryology of the Cardiovascular System
description: Development and developmental anomalies of the cardiovascular system.

title: Adult Echocardiography - Valvular Pathology
description: Introduction to cardiac pathology seen on echocardiograms in the adult population.

title: Clinical Education II - Echocardiography
description: Clinical practicum rotations including adult and pediatric echocardiography.

title: Pediatric Echocardiography I
description: Introduction to various types of congenital heart disease and pediatric cardiac procedures.

title: Adult Echocardiography II - Cardiomyopathies
description: Cardiovascular disease and diagnostic cardiac procedures in the adult population.

title: Clinical Education III - Echocardiography
description: Clinical practicum which includes adult and pediatric echocardiography clinical rotations with focus on advanced adult competencies.

title: Introduction to Radiologic Science and Healthcare
description: Introduction to Radiologic Technology imaging principles and equipment. Basic concepts of patient care, emergency care, CPR, medical ethics, legal issues, and cultural diversity.

title: Radiation Protection
description: All aspects of radiation protection including radiation safety, detection, and measurement relevant to Radiologic Technology.

title: Principles of Imaging I
description: Radiographic qualities and factors influencing those qualities. X-ray tube, beam restricting devices, exposure factors, grids and scatter radiation.

title: Radiographic Procedures I
description: Introduction to radiographic positioning of the chest, upper extremities, urinary and digestive systems. Pharmacology of contrast media.

title: Radiography Clinical Education I
description: Students rotate through all aspects of the radiologic science field. A total of 65 competencies must be achieved in two years of clinical education.

title: Radiation Biology
description: Effects of radiation from the cellular level to the entire human body. Long and short term somatic effects and genetic effects.

title: Principles of Imaging II
description: Radiographic qualities and the factors influencing those qualities. Systems and chemicals used in radiographic processing. Film and processor sensitometry.

title: Radiographic Procedures II
description: Introduction to radiographic positioning for lower extremities, spine, and digestive system. Students learn to identify anatomy on radiographs and evaluate them for acceptability.

title: Radiography Clinical Education II
description: Students rotate through all aspects of the radiologic science field. A total of 65 competencies must be achieved in two years of clinical education.

title: Radiographic Procedures III
description: Basic concepts of advanced imaging, special procedures, and supplementary imaging modalities.

title: Radiographic Clinical Education III
description: Clinical Education allows students to rotate through all aspects of the radiologic science field. A total of 65 competencies must be achieved in two years of clinical education.

title: Seminar in Radiography II
description: Evaluation of all aspects of the imaging system from processor to generator.

title: Introduction to MSK Sonography
description: Study of anatomy (to include anatomic variants), physiology, pathophysiology and the sonographic appearance of the musculoskeletal system as evaluated by diagnostic medical sonography.

title: Adult Echocardiography - Advanced Pathology
description: Cardiovascular pharmacology and the potential effects of cardiac medications on echocardiographic images.

title: Pediatric Echocardiography II
description: Advanced instruction in various types of congenital heart disease and pediatric echocardiography.

title: Stress Echocardiography
description: Indications, utility, limitations and technical procedures related to exercise stress echocardiography.

title: Clinical Education IV - Echocardiography
description: Clinical practicum rotations including advanced adult and pediatric imaging of the heart with focus on advanced pediatric competencies.

title: Clinical Education V - Echocardiography
description: Clinical practicum rotations including exercise and pharmacological stress, echocardiograms and transesophageal echocardiograms.

title: Clinical Education VI - Echocardiography
description: Clinical practicum including advanced imaging in echocardiography techniques.

title: Special Procedures in Echocardiography
description: Indications, utility, limitations and technical procedures related to transesophageal echocardiography.

title: Vascular Sonography I
description: Anatomy, physiology, pathology, and pathophysiology of the peripheral vascular subset of the cardiovascular system as it relates to sonography.

title: Vascular Sonography II
description: Anatomy, physiology, pathology, and pathophysiology of the cerebrovascular subset of the cardiovascular system as it relates to sonography.

title: Sonography Clinical Education IV
description: Clinical practicum including abdominal and OB/GYN imaging, and vascular technology.

title: Seminar in OB/GYN Sonography
description: The role of ultrasound in assisting with treatment as it relates to women's reproductive health and prenatal care.

title: Embryology of the Heart
description: Echocardiographic examination of the fetal heart including normal anatomy and congenital heart disease.

title: Vascular Sonography III
description: Doppler principles, spectral display, hemodynamics of abdominal, transplant organ, and penile vascular techniques; vascular statistics.

title: Sonography Clinical Education V
description: Clinical practicum including abdominal and OB/GYN imaging, and vascular technology.

title: Seminar in Advanced Medical Imaging
description: Comparison of normal and pathological anatomy cases combining diagnostic medical sonography, computed sonography and magnetic resonance imaging.

title: Seminar in Advanced Medical Imaging
description: Comparison of normal and pathological anatomy cases combining diagnostic medical sonography, computed sonography and magnetic resonance imaging.

title: Professional Development in Sonography - Seminar
description: Current trends in health care and sonography. Federal regulations, Medicare, managed care, professional organizations, and certication exam preparation.

title: Sonography Clinical Education VI
description: Clinical practicum including abdominal and OB/GYN imaging, and vascular technology.

title: Seminar in Education and Management in Medical Imaging
description: Problem solving of management issues relating to diagnostic medical sonography and exploring educational strategies employed in ultrasound education.

title: Seminar in Education and Management in Medical Imaging
description: Problem solving of management issues relating to diagnostic medical sonography and exploring educational strategies employed in ultrasound education.

title: Introduction to Pediatric Sonography/Neurosonography
description: Anatomy, physiology, pathology, pathophysiology and the sonographic appearance of the normal and abnormal fetal and neonatal brain.

title: Radiographic Physics
description: Atomic and subatomic theories, electrical theory, interaction of x-rays with matter, production of radiation, equipment maintenance, x-ray circuits, tubes, and cooling charts.

title: Digital Imaging
description: Radiographic qualities and factors influencing those qualities. Principles of flouroscopy, AEC, tomography, and other accessory equipment.

title: Imaging Procedures IV
description: Skull positioning advanced skills, special procedures, and supplementary imaging modalities.

title: Radiography Clinical Education IV
description: Students to rotate through all aspects of the radiologic science field. A total of 65 competencies must be achieved in two years of clinical education.

title: Seminar in Radiography III
description: Current concepts in radiologic technology.

title: Cross Sectional Anatomy
description: Structure and physiology of the human body as it appears in cross section. Diagrams, CT, and MRI images are utilized.

title: Radiologic Pathology
description: Changes that occur in disease and injury and their applications to radiography.

title: Radiography Clinical Education V
description: Students rotate through all aspects of the radiologic science field.  A total of 65 competencies must be achieved in two years of clinical education.

title: Seminar in Radiography IV
description: Current concepts in radiologic technology.

title: Radiography Clinical Internship I
description: 600 hours of clinical experience. The student will begin surgical and evening rotations.

title: Radiography Clinical Internship II
description: Rotations through the Emergency Department, diagnostic area outpatient and orthopedic clinics, CT, MRI, and interventional radiology.

title: Radiography Clinical Internship IV
description: 70 hours of clinical practice. Opportunity to rotate through any area of interest in a variety of imaging modalities.

title: Professional Development in Radiography
description: Federal regulations and legislation as they relate to the health care profession and radiology. Preparation for the certification exam.

title: Radiography Clinical Education VI
description: Students rotate through all aspects of the radiologic science field. A total of 65 competencies must be achieved in two years of clinical education.

title: Radiography Clinical Internship III
description: 400 hours of clinical practice. Opportunity to rotate through any area of interest in a variety of imaging modalities.

description: Exploration and discussion of volunteerism and leadership in professional associations, professional advocacy, emerging trends in certification, re-certification and career pathways related to career advancement.

description: Exploration and discussion of volunteerism and leadership in professional associations, professional advocacy, emerging trends in certification, re-certification and career pathways related to career advancement.

description: Theoretical and empirical contributions of social psychology to health.

title: Seminar in Child-Clinical Psychology
description: Diagnosis, etiology, therapy, and research in child-clinical psychology are discussed and criticized.

description: Required internship for students in the clinical psychology PhD program.

title: New Student Experience in Public Health
description: Students new to UWM develop academic and life skills for success, explore campus resources, and reflect on personal development and careers in kinesiology, nutritional sciences, and public health.

title: Introduction to Public Health
description: Explore the science that connects us all: public health. Informed by contemporary and socially relevant issues, public health investigates how just and equitable communities prevent disease so everyone, including future generations, can thrive.

title: Exploring Global Environmental Health
description: Introduction to the sciences underpinning the study and response to infectious, chemical, and physical environmental threats to human health within a global public health framework.

title: Public Health from Cells to Society I
description: Introduces public health as a holistic interdisciplinary field spanning cell to society. Emphasizes active learning about current public health problems from multilevel and lifecourse perspectives.

title: Public Health from Cells to Society II
description: Introduces multilevel societal drivers of health inequities over the lifecourse. Actively explores how social inequality gets under the skin to influence current public health problems.

title: Child and Adolescent Health
description: Review evidence-based child and adolescent health promotion in school, community, and clinical settings; Adolescent risk behaviors of special interest.

title: Public Health Internship Course
description: Students conceptualize, reflect upon, and apply the experiences of their public health internship. They will consider questions such as: 'How does my internship connect to my future career and community?'

title: Climate Change, the Environment and Human Health
description: Explores how climate change adversely affects human health from local to global contexts. Students will evaluate innovative solutions.

title: Study Abroad:
description: Designed to enroll students in UWM sponsored program before course work level, content, and credits are determined and/or in specially prepared program course work.

title: Health and Disease: Concepts and Contexts
description: Concepts of health and disease across social, historical, political, and cultural contexts, with particular attention to the workings of race, gender, and sexuality.

title: Foundations of Epidemiology
description: Introduces descriptive and analytic epidemiology, determinants of health and health inequities in populations, and basic application of quantitative epidemiologic methods to public health.

title: Adolescent Health & Development
description: A public health perspective on adolescent development and health/mental health promotion Readings, assignments and activities focus on drug use, sexuality, obesity, trauma, depression and violence.

title: Introduction to Health Disparities
description: An introduction to health inequalities as they pertain to racial and ethnic populations in the U.S. and an overview of key determinants of population health.

title: Foundations for Action in Public Health
description: History, perspectives, and theoretical foundations of taking action and making change in public health and related disciplines.

title: Green fish, green worms & more: animal models and their applications
description: An introduction to the field of applied public health biology where animal models are being utilized to determine how environmental contaminants influence human health.

title: Environmental Health and Disease
description: Investigates how the environment and/or environmental factors impact human health and population health with an emphasis on the developmental origins of disease.

title: Public Health Research Methods I
description: Introduction to public health qualitative and quantitative research methods and considerations for conducting ethical public health research.

title: Topics in Public Health:
description: Topics of current interest in Public Health for undergradates.

title: Comparative Health Systems: A Social Determinants Approach
description: Building on a conceptual foundation and diverse examples, introduces the US and international healthcare systems, and their role in improving population health and health equity.

title: True Lies: Consuming and Communicating Quantitative Information
description: Develops quantitative critical thinking skills, from elementary statistics to causal inference and data visualization. Examines how data are used in health-related sciences and mass-media reports.

title: Strategies for Action in Public Health
description: In-depth study of assessment and planning methods, theory application, and interdisciplinary community-engaged approaches and strategies for taking action to promote population health and health equity.

title: Program Implementation & Evaluation for a Healthy Society
description: Introduces concepts and methods for developing strategies of implementation and evaluation of health programs. Fundamental knowledge and skills for implementing and evaluating health promotion programs.

title: Public Health Research Methods II
description: Application of qualitative and quantitative methods, interpretation of findings and communication of results in public health research.

title: History of Scientific Reasoning in Global Public Health and Epidemiology
description: Introduction to the historical development of public health and epidemiology, and how historical and social contexts shape scientific theories of disease distribution and determinants.

title: Public Health Research Using Social Media
description: Hands-on research and discussion seminar in which students examine a selected issue tied to social media and public health.

title: Public Health Integrative Experience (Service Learning)
description: Culmination of public health major. Integrates and applies core public health competencies through an experiential service learning placement in a community organization, and seminar-style discussions.

title: Independent Study
description: Independent study on a topic not available as a regular course.

title: Structures of Inequality and Population Health
description: Foundations of public health, critical social theory, and social justice praxis, which provide essential interdisciplinary tools for analyzing sociostructural processes and advancing social and health equity.

title: Public Health Principles and Practice
description: Examination of fundamental principles designed to improve the health of the public, public health theories, domains, and practices.

title: Introduction to Biostatistics
description: Development and application of statistical reasoning and methods in addressing, analyzing and solving problems in public health. Includes lab section with introduction to SAS, including macros and core statistical analysis functions.

title: Environmental Health Sciences
description: Survey of effects environment has on humans, and effects humans have on environment, emphasis on toxicology and infectious disease.

title: Principles and Methods of Epidemiology
description: Quantitative study of patterns and determinants of health in human populations applying biomedical and social epidemiology perspectives. Problem-based lab includes surveillance, measurement, study design, and causal inference applications.

title: Principles of Public Health Policy and Administration
description: The role of policy in influencing population health, policies that promote public health, the policymaking process, and the planning and administration of health systems.

title: Perspectives on Community  & Behavioral Health
description: Philosophical underpinnings, conceptual frameworks, and strategies for the application of behavioral and social science concepts to the goals of public health.

title: Health Systems and Population Health
description: Using fundamental concepts of health systems design, international comparisons, and case studies, this course demonstrates strategies through which health systems could improve health and reduce inequities by addressing social vulnerabilities.

title: Seminar in Biostatistics and Bioinformatics
description: This course provides general approaches and collection of biostatistics and bioinformatics methods and practice used in public health.

title: Intermediate Biostatistics
description: Introduction to modern multivariable statistical analysis, based on generalized linear models. Topics include linear regression, logistic regression, one-way and two-way ANOVA, longitudinal analysis, missing data, and mixed models.

title: Probability and Statistical Inference
description: Introductory graduate-level course that provides students with a mathematical treatment and understanding of key concepts in probability and distribution theory and statistical inference, and their applications in public health.

title: Statistical Genetics and Genetic Epidemiology
description: Introduction to statistical methods for the analysis of family and population based genetic data, including methods can be used in linkage analysis, family-based and population-based association studies.

title: Applied Categorical Data
description: Data analysis techniques for various kinds of categorical data for public health related examples using SAS.

title: Applied Survival Analysis
description: This course covers basic concepts and techniques in the statistical analysis of survival data.

title: Applied Longitudinal Data Analysis
description: This course will cover data analysis techniques for longitudinal data with focus on application in public health with related examples using SAS.

title: Data Management and Visualization in R
description: This course covers basic concepts and techniques for statistical programming with the R computing language.

title: Social Justice in Public Health
description: This course is designed to introduce you to the major social variables (e.g., socioeconomic status, race, poverty, social support, neighborhood environment) that affect public health.

title: Special Topics in Biostatistics:
description: Survey of an area in Biostatistics. Specific credits and add'l prereqs announced in Schedule of Classes each time course is offered.

title: Introduction to Translational Bioinformatics
description: Review bioinformatics knowledge and analytical skills, high-throughput technologies that produce various omic data, along with the methodologies to analyze and interpret different layers of information.

title: An Introduction to Bayesian Statistics
description: In parallel to classical statistical frequentist approach, Bayesian statistics serves another way to make statistical inference, which not only rely on the sample information collected, but also on external prior knowledge.

title: Design, Conduct and Analysis of Clinical Trials
description: Introduction to the design, conduct and analysis of phase I-IV clinical trials, with an emphasis on phase III trials and ethical issues in clinical research.

title: Data Analysis & Scientific Communication Incorporating Use of Generative AI
description: Focuses on analyzing real-world data and communicating biostatistical results effectively. Generative AI tools are used to support appropriate statistical analysis and interpretation without requiring advanced programming skills.

title: Theories and Models of Health Behavior
description: Examine theories of health behavior targeted to each level of the social ecological model, including historical and public health context. Assess utility of these theories in various domains.

title: Community Health Assessment
description: Introduction to the concepts and techniques of community health assessment; conducting and critically analyzing community assessments.

title: Program Planning & Implementation in Public Health
description: Systematic approach to planning and implementing public health programs, examining program monitoring, methods of impact assessment, and measuring efficiency.

title: Program Evaluation in Public Health
description: Students design and present research and evaluation plans, receive guidance on developing conceptual frameworks and hypotheses, collecting and analyzing data, and developing program evaluation plans.

title: Survey Research Methods in Public Health
description: The application of survey methods with emphases on sampling, survey design and planning, and data collection procedures.

title: Overview of Children with Special Health Care Needs
description: Overview of the complex issues affecting children with special health care needs and their families, including access/use of health programs and other services, policies, interventions, financing, and coordination/transition of care.

title: Overview of Qualitative Methods for Public Health
description: Introduction to qualitative methods for public health.

title: Special Topics in Public Health:
description: Topics of current interest in public health.

title: Environmental Public Health Microbiology
description: Environmental health microbiology is the study of microbial processes in water, land, and food that affect human health.

title: Environmental Risk Assessment
description: Risk assessment practices from an environmental health perspective, complexities and challenges of regulation, management, and mitigation of risks for both human and ecosystem health.

title: Developmental Toxicology
description: An introduction to the field of developmental toxicology and how environmental contaminants influence vertebrate development, including humans.

title: Seminar in Environmental Health Sciences:
description: Survey of an area in environmental health.

title: Public Health and Mental Health
description: Understanding mental health and mental illness from a public health perspective; designed for an interdisciplinary audience of students, researchers and practitioners.

title: Social Epidemiology
description: Foundations of social epidemiology, including key concepts, theoretical frameworks, and methods for studying social and structural determinants of population health and health inequity.

title: Intro to Regression for Understanding the SDOH
description: Using STATA on real data to build regression models, perform diagnostics, and interpret results. Utilize social theory to put results into context.

title: Epidemiology Field Methods
description: Prepares students to conduct epidemiologic field studies. Introduction of reviewing literature, designing questionnaires, developing surveillance systems, conducting emergency outbreak investigations, applying for IRB approval, and public relations.

title: Environmental Epidemiology
description: Expands upon basic epidemiological principles to tackle current problems in studies of health impacts of contaminants in air, water, food supply, consumer products, and indoor spaces, emphasizing a cross-disciplinary approach.

title: Epidemiology for Equity
description: Students will integrate epidemiology methods with the principles, methods, and skills of participatory action research aimed at structural change to promote social and/or environmental justice and health equity.

title: Cancer Epidemiology
description: The course will provide an introduction to cancer epidemiology and prevention evaluating methods to study cancer etiology and survival with a particular focus on understanding cancer disparities.

title: Critical Perspectives on Nutritional Epidemiology and the Food System
description: This course offers a synthesis of nutritional epidemiological methods and food systems perspectives to build students critical understanding of diets, nutrition, and their public health implications.

title: Qualitative Approaches in Public Health Policy
description: Introduces students to foundational approaches to qualitative research for use in public health policy and administration. Provides opportunities to practice foundational data collection, research analysis, reflexivity, and research design skills.

title: Quantitative Research Methods for Public Health Policy
description: Introduces quantitative methods commonly used in public health policy and administration decision-making, skills to analyze quantitative research and to apply knowledge generated from research to the public health policy and administration context.

title: Public Health Policymaking and Policy Analysis
description: Introduces students to key frameworks for public health policymaking and policy analysis. Students apply concepts to a real world public health problem of their choice.

title: Seminar in Public Health Policy and Administration
description: Survey of an area in Public Health Policy and Administration. Specific credits and add'l prereqs announced in Schedule of Classes each time course offered.

title: Public Health Management
description: Basic concepts, best-practice leadership, and management principles with the emphasis on organizational behavior in public health settings.

title: Social and Economic Policy as Health Policy
description: This course examines how social and economic policies, including education, income re-distribution, and housing shape population health. A broad overview of the social determinants of health is introduced/reviewed and current knowledge of the impact.

title: Principles of Public Health Economics
description: The course introduces the application of microeconomic theories and models to explain major topics and trends in public health and health care settings.

title: Leadership in Public Health

title: Capstone in Public Health
description: Application of acquired public health knowledge, experience and competencies in developing a public health project that demonstrates readiness for professional practice. Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory only.

title: Seminar in Public Health Research
description: Immersion in interdisciplinary collaborative approaches to public health research.

title: Advanced Epidemiology
description: Advanced training in epidemiological concepts, principles and methods in the context of population health and health equity.

title: Writing a Federal Grant in the Public Health Sciences
description: This course is designed to provide students a hands-on experience with all aspects of preparation of a hypothesis driven grant application to a federal agency such as the NIH or NSF.

title: Fundamentals and Applications of Structural Equation Modeling
description: Introduction to the fundamental concepts, methodologies, and applications of Structural Equation Modeling (SEM), a powerful statistical framework for analyzing complex relationships among observed and latent variables at the graduate level.

title: Causal Inference
description: This course will cover a variety of approaches to define and estimate causal effects, as well as the sensitivity analysis for both clinical trial and observational studies.

title: Statistical Learning & Data Mining
description: Analysis of data using modern data mining technique.

title: Statistical Computing
description: This course will cover the theory and application of common algorithms used in statistical computing.

title: Social and Environmental Justice in Public Health
description: Social and environmental justice perspective on public health problems and concerns.

title: Maternal and Child Health Foundations, Policy and Practice
description: The foundations of MCH, historical context, financing, challenges, and opportunities in advancing MCH at state, national and international level including the integration of men.

title: Advanced Survey of Environmental Health
description: The role of environmental factors in determining human health, and human processes that degrade this interaction.

title: Applied Analysis of Binary Outcomes in Public Health Research

description: Seminar covering classics in community-based public health research and the development of conceptual and methodological skills in community engagement.

title: Research Design in Community and Behavioral Health Promotion
description: Examination of experimental, quasi-experimental, and nonexperimental study designs, focus groups, and coding with qualitative software.

title: Leadership and Advocacy in Maternal and Child Health
description: Overview of leadership, negotiation, and advocacy theory, skills, and styles in maternal and child health (MCH) and public health and their applications in policies, practice, and interventions to promote health equity.

title: Community Engagement and Participatory Research Approaches in Public Health
description: Effective approaches to engaging communities in health interventions and addressing health disparities.

title: Racial/Ethnic Health Disparities in the United States
description: The course will analyze studies of racial/ethnic health disparities, with a focus to include multi-level and temporal perspectives to better understand the social context in which the determinants of racial/ethnic health are embedded.

title: Research Ethics in Epidemiology and Public Health
description: Training in research ethics and ethical analysis in the context of public health and epidemiology research. Covers all topics outlined in the NIH's Responsible Conduct of Research Training.

title: Critical Methodologies for Health Equity Research
description: Analyzes interdisciplinary critical methodologies for researching health inequities across diverse axes of power and considers how to integrate these methodologies into epidemiologic and public health research to advance health equity.

title: Epidemiologic Links Between Infectious and Chronic Disease
description: Discussion of social patterning of infection, epidemiologic and physiologic links between infections and chronic disease, and methodological considerations in the evaluation of such associations.

title: Epidemiology in Health Policy and Advocacy
description: Using epidemiologic evidence to inform public health policy, through engagement with the policy process, communication with policymakers, and evidence-based appraisal of policy alternatives and advocacy for sound policy.

title: Applied Biostatistics Research Project
description: Conduct in-depth statistical analysis and compile a comprehensive research project report under the guidance of assigned faculty.

title: Survey of Analytic Methods for Epidemiology
description: Survey of advanced analytic approaches to the conduct of epidemiologic research in the pursuit of causal inference.

title: Generalized Linear Models
description: This course will cover theory of advanced biostatistics models with focus on generalized linear models and will also cover generalized estimating equation, generalized linear mixed models. We will emphasize implementation in R.

description: These seminars are intended as professional development to prepare students for successful careers in the area of Community and Health Behavior Promotion.

title: Core Doctoral Seminar in Epidemiology
description: This advanced doctoral seminar discusses practical aspects of an epidemiology career, from developing a research question and study, to managing a grant, writing results and reporting results to the press.

description: Sociology as a special field of social science. The relationship of the work of the sociologist to contemporary society; an examination of the sociological perspective.
